'''Vegath Tist''' was a [[Salt/Legends|salt]]-addicted [[Gender/Legends|male]] [[Arcona/Legends|Arcona]] and an outcast from his family. A speculator, Tist spent much of his life traveling [[The galaxy/Legends|the galaxy]] dabbling in get-rich schemes and questionable endeavors in an effort to make a fortune and keep himself supplied with salt. Trading on his skills as a [[Mining/Legends|miner]], he eventually made his way to [[Kal'Shebbol/Legends|Kal'Shebbol]], the [[Capital/Legends|capital]] of the [[Kathol sector/Legends|Kathol sector]] in the [[Outer Rim Territories/Legends|Outer Rim Territories]]. Shortly after the [[Battle of Kal'Shebbol|liberation]] of the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] by [[New Republic/Legends|New Republic]] forces in [[8 ABY/Legends|8 ABY]], he signed onto the crew of the [[CR90 corvette/Legends|CR90 corvette]] ''[[FarStar]]'' as a [[Mineralogist/Legends|mineralogist]]. The [[Starship/Legends|ship]] was on a [[DarkStryder Campaign|mission]] to track down the former ruler of Kal'Shebbol, [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Imperial]] [[Warlord/Legends|warlord]] [[Moff/Legends|Moff]] [[Kentor Sarne]], and neutralize the threat he still posed to the region. Tist served on the ship and participated in the [[Battle of Kathol System|Battle of Kathol]], helping to liberate the [[Kathol (planet)|planet]] from its ruler, the [[Bioengineering/Legends|bio-engineered]] [[Insectoid/Legends|insectoid]] known as the [[DarkStryder]], and defeat Sarne and his [[Moff Sarne's forces|forces]]. Tist stayed on the planet following the conflict, hoping to learn a [[Ta-Ree]] ability—a manifestation of [[The Force/Legends|the Force]] practiced by the native inhabitants—to cure him of his salt addiction.

==Biography==
===Early life===
Vegath Tist, a [[Gender/Legends|male]] [[Arcona/Legends|Arcona]], was addicted to [[Salt/Legends|salt]],<ref name="tdc" /> a common condiment that acted as a powerful hallucinogen to members of his [[Species/Legends|species]].<ref name="GG4">[[Galaxy Guide 4: Alien Races (Second Edition)|''Galaxy Guide 4: Alien Races'', Second Edition]]</ref> He required a daily consumption of at least twenty-five [[gram]]s, lest he begin to suffer from the early stages of withdrawal. At some point prior to [[8 ABY/Legends|8 ABY]], he was cast out and shunned by his own family for an unspecified deed, although his intense addiction may have been the cause. Tist began to travel [[The galaxy/Legends|the galaxy]], seeking his fortune by involving himself in various get-rich-quick schemes and investing what [[Galactic Credit Standard/Legends|money]] he made into scams that always failed. He portrayed himself as a [[Geologist/Legends|geologist]] by profession and, while he did possess some [[Mining/Legends|mining]] skills, he was hardly the expert he claimed to be.<ref name="tdc" />

Eventually, Tist ended up in the [[Mestra system/Legends|Mestra system]],<ref name="tdc" /> in the [[Minos Cluster/Legends|Minos Cluster]] of the [[Outer Rim Territories/Legends|Outer Rim Territories]],<ref name="TEA-OC">{{SW|url=news/star-wars-the-essential-atlas-online-companion|text=''Star Wars: The Essential Atlas'' Online Companion|int=Star Wars: The Essential Atlas Online Companion}}</ref> which had one of the largest [[Asteroid field/Legends|asteroid belts]] in the galaxy and was rich in [[Durelium/Legends|durelium]] [[Ore/Legends|ore]].<ref name="gg6">[[Galaxy Guide 6: Tramp Freighters (Second Edition)|''Galaxy Guide 6: Tramp Freighters'', Second Edition]]</ref> He was forced to mine the [[Asteroid/Legends|asteroids]] as an indentured servant but was eventually able to flee the [[Star system/Legends|system]] with whatever he had facility to steal. Making the short journey from the Minos Cluster to the [[Kathol sector/Legends|Kathol sector]], Tist ended up on the [[Capital/Legends|capital]] [[Planet/Legends|planet]], [[Kal'Shebbol/Legends|Kal'Shebbol]], where he fell in with a local [[Crime lord/Legends|crime boss]]. His [[Time/Legends|time]] as a miner and geologist had given him extensive experience with explosives, and he traded his knowledge and skills for salt to feed his addiction.<ref name="tdc" />

===Serving aboard the ''FarStar''===
The [[New Republic/Legends|New Republic]] [[Battle of Kal'Shebbol|liberated]] Kal'Shebbol in 8 ABY and drove the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Imperial]] [[Warlord/Legends|warlord]] [[Moff/Legends|Moff]] [[Kentor Sarne]] from the planet. However, Sarne had escaped the planet with the bulk of his [[Moff Sarne's forces|fleet]], and the New Republic organized a [[DarkStryder Campaign|mission]] to track him down. Forced to utilize a heavily modified [[CR90 corvette/Legends|CR90 corvette]] named the ''[[FarStar]]'' that had previously served in Sarne's [[Fleet/Legends|fleet]], the New Republic was unable to spare much manpower to crew the [[Starship/Legends|vessel]], so an open call was put out to the civilian population of Kal'Shebbol to assist in the venture. Seeing an opportunity to get out from under the influence of the crime boss, and the possibility of making some money, Tist signed on to the mission. He claimed to be a [[Mineralogist/Legends|mineralogist]] to improve his chances of being selected for the mission.<ref name="tdc" />

[[File:FarStar.jpg|thumb|300px|The ''FarStar'']]
Tist served on the ''FarStar'' during its mission through the wilderness of the Kathol [[Sector/Legends|sector]], assisting the expedition in any way he could. [[Standard month/Legends|Months]] after launching from Kal'Shebbol, the ''FarStar'' tracked Sarne to the [[Kathol system/Legends|Kathol system]], where the Moff had gathered the bulk of his forces. Sarne had allied himself with the [[DarkStryder]], a [[Bioengineering/Legends|bio-engineered]] [[Insectoid/Legends|insectoid]] entity that ruled the system's main planet, [[Kathol (planet)|Kathol]], in a mutually beneficial pact. The DarkStryder wanted [[Hyperspace/Legends|hyperspace]] technology so that it could leave Kathol, while Sarne wanted the [[DarkStryder technology|exotic technology]] that the DarkStryder produced to augment his military forces and wage war against his enemies. To land on the planet Kathol undetected and investigate both Sarne's presence on the surface and the DarkStryder itself, ''FarStar'' [[Captain/Legends|Captain]] [[Kaiya Adrimetrum]] decided to simulate a [[Meteorite/Legends|meteor]] shower by redirecting some of the system's asteroids, then using them as cover for the ''FarStar''{{'s}} descent. Tist was part of the team, along with the [[Guudrian]] pyromaniac [[Boom]], that primed several asteroids with explosives to nudge them out of their [[Orbit/Legends|orbits]] and toward the planet, using his knowledge of demolitions and mining techniques. The tactic was successful, and the [[Corvette/Legends|corvette]] made planetfall undetected.<ref name="e">''[[Endgame]]''</ref>

A member of one of the initial landing and [[Scout/Legends|reconnaissance]] parties sent out by Adrimetrum, Tist met with several of the indigenous species. The recon teams learned that many of the species on Kathol were the result of [[Bioengineering/Legends|genetic engineering]] experiments by the DarkStryder, who had created each species on Kathol to serve it in some way, and each was designed to fill a certain role. Because of Tist's Arconan physiology, several of the tribes believed that he was a new and superior product of the DarkStryder's genetic engineering programs, and they regarded him with awe. After Adrimetrum succeeded in uniting several of the indigenous species into a coalition to oppose Sarne and the tyrannical DarkStryder, Tist participated in the subsequent [[Battle of Kathol System|Battle of Kathol]]. Despite the destruction of the ''FarStar'', the New Republic force liberated the planet from the control of the DarkStryder and [[Death/Legends|ended the threat]] of Moff Sarne.<ref name="e" />

{{RPGseed}}
Following the battle, Tist remained on Kathol as the honored guest of one of the native [[Yapi]] tribes, a genetically-bred warrior race. Having learned of [[Ta-Ree]] [[Magic/Legends|magic]], which was a manifestation of [[The Force/Legends|the Force]] unique to the planet, Tist hoped to learn an ability that would cure him of his addiction to salt.<ref name="e" />
{{Endgame}}

==Personality and traits==
Vegath Tist, an [[orange]]-skinned Arcona, was addicted to salt, a trait common among his species, and obtaining more of the substance dictated the majority of his actions. Due to his addiction, his [[Eye/Legends|eyes]] turned [[Gold/Legends|gold]] in [[Color/Legends|color]], which showed his problem to those around him. He carried his stash in a pouch around his neck and constantly fiddled with it. He was easily exploited if promised more salt; the crime boss he worked for on Kal'Shebbol gained Tist's knowledge of explosives in exchange for the supply of a common condiment. When he left Kal'Shebbol onboard the ''FarStar'', Tist had one [[Kilogram/Legends|kilogram]] of salt in his pouch, enough for forty days at his minimum level of consumption, although he was confident of resupplying his stash during the mission.<ref name="tdc" /> Despite that, Tist knew that his situation was no good for him, and he finally sought a way to break his dependency on it, even if it was to put his faith in Ta-Ree teachings.<ref name="e" />

Tist was also unafraid to bend the truth slightly, exaggerating his own skills and talents to make himself seem more experienced than he really was. This allowed him to take positions and take advantage of opportunities to which he may not have normally have had access. Tist was always looking for the quick payoff, or a way to earn credits quickly, and often put his faith in suspect endeavors.<ref name="tdc" />

Working as a miner, Tist learned how to survive in mines, and came to recognize the value of minerals. He was aware of other planetary systems, and could operate [[Sensor/Legends|sensor]] and security equipment, program and repair [[Computer/Legends|computers]], and handle [[Bomb/Legends|explosives]]. He had experience with [[Melee weapon/Legends|melee combat]], and carried a [[Shovel/Legends|shovel]] and survey [[Datapad/Legends|datapad]].<ref name="tdc" />

==Behind the scenes==
Vegath Tist was created for ''[[The DarkStryder Campaign]]'', a ''[[Star Wars: The Roleplaying Game]]'' supplement, as a member of the ''FarStar''{{'s}} support crew. He was not featured much in the following supplements—''[[The Kathol Outback]]'' and the [[Kathol Rift/Legends|''Kathol Rift'']]—but gained several mentions in the final sourcebook of the [[The DarkStryder Campaign (series)|series]], ''[[Endgame]]''. Although players were free to develop Tist as they wished during the campaign, both ''The DarkStryder Campaign'' and ''The Kathol Rift'' specifically stated that he was to be kept alive during the events of the campaign, since he would be developed further in ''Endgame''. In the "Gamesmaster Crew Notes" section of ''Endgame'', Tist's entry states that a "Suggested Development" for the character would be to remain on Kathol and try to cure his addiction to salt by studying the Ta-Ree teachings. This article assumes that Tist remained on the planet as suggested in the sourcebook. Tist's name was later mentioned in the [[2003]] sourcebook ''[[Ultimate Alien Anthology]]'', as an example Arcona name.
